interviewStages: Behavioral: customer focus, fast-moving culture, ownership
codingTopics: String templating and parsing for email rendering
Streams: send/open/click event processing, sliding-window engagement metrics
Hash-map dedupe and merge of subscriber lists
Queues and scheduling: campaign sends under rate limits, heap-based greedy
Rails-style OO design and time-series analytics
systemDesignTopics: Design a newsletter-send pipeline that delivers 100 million emails an hour with per-domain rate limits
Design the subscriber-segmentation engine that evaluates dynamic audience rules at send time
Design an email open- and click-tracking system that stays accurate under bot and prefetch traffic

coding: Dedupe a merged subscriber list by email with normalization rules (case, dots, plus-addressing); design the hash-map keys.
Render a newsletter template by substituting {{tokens}} with subscriber fields, handling missing fields and HTML escaping.
From a stream of send and open events, compute open rate per campaign over a sliding 24-hour window.
Schedule N campaigns across per-domain rate limits so all complete soonest — greedy with a heap; prove it terminates.
Detect bot opens: subscribers whose opens arrive within one second of send for k consecutive campaigns.
behavioral: beehiiv prizes bias to action. Tell me about shipping something end-to-end fast — what did you consciously skip?
Describe owning quality with no QA team: how do you test what you ship?
Tell me about handling rapid context switching across features at a small startup.
Describe a time user feedback changed what you built mid-stream.
systemDesign: Design bulk email sending: queueing, ESP/IP warm-up, per-domain throttles, and bounce handling.
Design the referral-program feature: attribution, fraud checks, and reward fulfillment.
Design newsletter analytics that creators actually trust: opens, clicks, and bot filtering.
values: beehiiv is creator-first. Tell me about building something where the user's income depended on your reliability.
We move fast with high expectations for speed and quality. When have you delivered both, and what did it cost?
Why newsletters and the creator economy — why does this space matter?
other: How does Beehiiv compare to Substack?

Updated May 5, 2026 · techinterview.org

⏱ 1 min read

Beehiiv
beehiiv.com ↗

Beehiiv is the modern newsletter platform competing with Substack, founded by ex-Morning Brew employees. Used by tens of thousands of newsletters. The interview is small-team and rewards engineers excited about the creator economy and modern web stack.

Process

Recruiter screen → 60-minute coding pair → onsite virtual: 2 coding, 1 system design, 1 craft deep-dive, 1 behavioral. Cycle: 2–4 weeks.

What they actually ask

- Design a newsletter delivery and analytics pipeline

- Design referral / growth viral loops (similar to Morning Brew's growth playbook)

- Design ad serving network for newsletter monetization

- Coding: practical Ruby/Rails or TypeScript

- Behavioral: customer focus, fast-moving culture, ownership

Levels and comp (2026)

- SE: $160K–$210K total

- Senior SE: $230K–$310K

- Staff: $330K–$440K

- Principal: $460K–$610K

Equity has high upside given Series B funding and category leadership.

Prep priorities

- Be fluent in Ruby on Rails (primary stack)

- Understand growth loops, referral mechanics, and viral marketing

- Brush up on email deliverability and ad-serving patterns

Frequently Asked Questions

Is Beehiiv remote-friendly?

Hybrid in NYC (HQ); some remote within US for senior+ roles.

How does Beehiiv compare to Substack?

Substack has the larger brand and creator network. Beehiiv has more features, faster shipping, and stronger growth-tooling. Comp is comparable; bar is high at both.

What is the engineering culture?

Fast-moving, ship-focused, ownership-heavy. Younger team than Substack. Strong fit for engineers who like startup intensity.
